_note: Hand-authored. State ids carry no label in the settings file and there is no ESI or fsdbinary source; the id->label map lives in client script. Derived positionally: EVE's Overview Settings > Appearance > Background list renders in exactly backgroundOrder2 order, so a screenshot maps row-by-row onto that array. Verified against stateColors: all 13 stored colours match the swatch on their mapped row. Ids 36/37 pinned by live experiment (see docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-22-overview-states-colors-tags.md Task 1). Id 68 is present in the order arrays but never rendered by the client and is deliberately unnamed.

states.9: Pilot has a security status below -5
states.10: Pilot has a security status below 0
states.12: Pilot is in your Capsuleer corporation
states.13: Pilot is at war with your corporation/alliance
states.44: Pilot is at war with your militia
states.45: Pilot is in your militia or allied to your militia
states.48: Pilot is in your Non Capsuleer corporation
states.49: Pilot is an ally in one or more of your wars
states.52: Pilot has a limited engagement with you
states.53: Pilot has a kill right on them that you can activate

_defaultStatesNote: Both default*States arrays are EVE's own output: an account whose overview settings were reset in-game wrote exactly these 21 ids to backgroundStates2 AND flagStates2, and wrote the two order arrays below byte-for-byte as they appear here. Note 66 (retribution timer) is present in the ORDER arrays but NOT enabled by default — the earlier assumption that the default is 'everything enabled' was wrong.
_defaultColorsNote: EVE's built-in per-state background colour, shown for states the file stores no override for. NOT in any settings file (stateColors holds only overrides; restoreData's stateColorsNameList mirrors the same overrides as palette names; defaultoverview holds only a profile id) — it lives in client script, so these are sampled pixel-exact from EVE's own Overview Settings > Appearance > Background swatches on an account whose overview settings had just been reset in-game (a reset restores the built-ins, and the restored list came back in exactly this file's defaultBackgroundOrder — an independent confirmation of that array). Sampling is trustworthy: on a second, customised account, rows that DID store a colour rendered the file's floats exactly (BF0000 = 0.75/0/0, 002699 = 0/0.15/0.6, 337FFF = 0.2/0.5/1, FF5900 = 1/0.35/0, B2B2B2 = 0.7/0.7/0.7), and the nine rows that account left unset matched this table on eight of nine. Only the palette's six colours appear: red, orange, purple, darkBlue, blue, white, plus green for 48 and black for 66. Display only: an entry here is never written to a file. An id absent here falls back to a neutral placeholder.
